de.cm-01.description: Review and remove unused EC2 security groups to reduce complexity in network management and improve network security and performance.

de.cm-01.description: Change VPC default security groups rules from their default configuration and ensure defaults are not used to prevent unrestricted inbound/outbound traffic.

de.cm-01.title: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones
de.cm-01.description: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones to provide redundancy, scalability and improved availability.

de.cm-01.title: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in AWS Network Firewall policies
de.cm-01.description: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in Network Firewall policies that match defined control parameters.

de.cm-01.title: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancers
de.cm-01.description: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancer to customize security settings according to specific requirements and specific cipher standards.

de.cm-01.title: Implement network segmentation with VPC and Security Groups
de.cm-01.description: Use Amazon VPC to create isolated network segments and Security Groups to control traffic flow between resources

de.cm-01.title: Use AWS Network Firewall for stateful traffic inspection
de.cm-01.description: Deploy AWS Network Firewall to inspect and filter traffic between VPCs and to/from the internet

de.cm-01.title: Implement VPC Flow Logs for traffic monitoring
de.cm-01.description: Enable VPC Flow Logs to capture information about IP traffic going to and from network interfaces

de.cm-03.description: Configure AWS CloudTrail to log all API calls and management events across your AWS account

de.cm-03.description: Enable VPC Flow Logs to capture detailed information about network traffic for security analysis

de.cm-03.title: Enable AWS Config for resource configuration auditing
de.cm-03.description: Use AWS Config to record configuration changes and evaluate compliance against desired configurations

de.cm-03.title: Enable MFA for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that have a console password
de.cm-03.description: Enable MFA for IAM users that have a console password in order to strengthen user authentication.

de.cm-03.title: Ensure all AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user groups contain users
de.cm-03.description: Ensure that all IAM user groups contain users, or remove empty user groups when no longer needed so the groups cannot be used to unintentionally grant access.

de.cm-03.title: Manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) identities
de.cm-03.description: Manage access in AWS by creating and attaching IAM policies to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to data and resources.

de.cm-03.title: Configure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users
de.cm-03.description: Configure MFA for IAM users to protect resources from unauthorized access.

de.cm-03.title: Prevent IAM inline policies from using wildcard permissions in both Action and Resource elements
de.cm-03.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from including 'Effect' 'Allow' with 'Action' '*' over 'Resource' '*' to enforce least privilege access.

de.cm-03.title: Enforce IAM password policy to require at least one uppercase letter
de.cm-03.description: Enforces that IAM password policies require at least one uppercase letter in passwords, strengthening authentication security against brute force attacks.

de.cm-03.title: Enforce strong password policies for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
de.cm-03.description: Requires IAM user password policies to implement strong configurations, preventing the use of weak passwords and reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

de.cm-03.title: Enforce IAM password policy to require at least one lowercase letter
de.cm-03.description: Enforces that IAM password policies require at least one lowercase letter in passwords to strengthen authentication security.

de.cm-03.title: Ensure IAM users do not have policies directly attached in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
de.cm-03.description: Enforce IAM users to inherit permissions from IAM groups or roles rather than having inline or managed policies directly attached.

de.cm-03.title: Restrict IAM inline policies from using wildcard service actions in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
de.cm-03.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from using 'Effect' 'Allow' with wildcard service actions to enforce least privilege access.

de.cm-09.title: Implement AWS Signer for code signing
de.cm-09.description: Use AWS Signer to digitally sign code and verify software integrity

de.cm-09.title: Enable EC2 Instance Metadata Service v2 (IMDSv2)
de.cm-09.description: Use IMDSv2 to protect instance metadata from unauthorized access and ensure integrity

de.cm-09.title: Disable automatic assignment of public IP addresses for Amazon VPC subnets
de.cm-09.description: Disable automatic assignment of public IP addresses for Amazon VPC subnets to prevent instances from being accessed directly from the public Internet.

de.cm-09.title: Restrict security group public Internet ingress traffic sources for remote access services
de.cm-09.description: Prevent unauthorized access to EC2 resources by configuring security groups to restrict public Internet ingress traffic sources for remote access services.

de.cm-09.title: Remove unused Amazon EC2 security groups
de.cm-09.description: Review and remove unused EC2 security groups to reduce complexity in network management and improve network security.

gv.oc-01.title: Enterprise Architecture
gv.oc-01.implementation_guidance: Design multi-account architecture with Organizations. Implement landing zone with Control Tower. Use Service Catalog for approved architectures. Define security zones and network segmentation. Document reference architectures. Include security in architecture review board.

gv.oc-01.title: Risk Management Strategy
gv.oc-01.implementation_guidance: Document risk management framework. Use Security Hub for risk visibility. Enable GuardDuty for threat detection. Run Inspector for vulnerability assessment. Define risk appetite and tolerance. Implement risk register in DynamoDB. Review risks quarterly.

gv.oc-01.title: Mission/Business Process Definition
gv.oc-01.implementation_guidance: Document business processes supported by AWS. Map processes to AWS services and accounts. Define criticality and recovery objectives. Use Service Catalog to provision approved solutions. Document process dependencies. Include in business continuity planning.

gv.oc-02.title: Information Security Program Plan
gv.oc-02.description: Develop and maintain information security program plan
gv.oc-02.implementation_guidance: Document enterprise-wide security program. Use AWS Organizations for multi-account governance. Enable Security Hub across all accounts. Use Audit Manager for compliance tracking. Define security roles and responsibilities. Establish security metrics and reporting. Review program plan annually.

gv.oc-02.title: Senior Information Security Officer
gv.oc-02.implementation_guidance: Designate CISO or equivalent role. Grant appropriate IAM permissions for security oversight. Use Organizations for centralized security management. Document security officer responsibilities. Establish reporting structure. Provide access to Security Hub, GuardDuty, and audit tools.

gv.oc-02.implementation_guidance: Design multi-account architecture with Organizations. Implement landing zone with Control Tower. Use Service Catalog for approved architectures. gv.oc-03.title: Planning Policy and Procedures
gv.oc-03.implementation_guidance: Document security planning procedures. Define system authorization process. Establish security architecture requirements. Store policies in S3 with versioning. Review policies annually. Communicate policies to stakeholders.

gv.oc-03.title: Rules of Behavior
gv.oc-03.description: Establish rules of behavior for system users
gv.oc-03.implementation_guidance: Document acceptable use policies for AWS resources. Present rules during user onboarding. Use Cognito pre-authentication triggers to require acceptance. Store signed acknowledgments in S3. Include rules for: password management, MFA usage, data handling, incident reporting, remote access. Review and update rules annually.

gv.po-01.title: Planning Policy and Procedures
gv.po-01.description: Establish planning policies
gv.po-01.implementation_guidance: Document security planning procedures. Define system authorization process. Establish security architecture requirements. Store policies in S3 with versioning. Review policies annually. Communicate policies to stakeholders.

gv.po-01.title: Document IAM policies and procedures
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented access control policies that define IAM user management, role-based access, and least privilege principles.

gv.po-01.title: Use AWS Organizations for policy enforcement
gv.po-01.description: Implement Service Control Policies (SCPs) in AWS Organizations to enforce access control policies across all accounts.

gv.po-01.title: Review IAM Access Analyzer findings
gv.po-01.description: Regularly review IAM Access Analyzer findings to identify unintended access to resources and ensure policy compliance.

gv.po-01.title: Security Awareness and Training Policy
gv.po-01.description: Establish security awareness and training policies
gv.po-01.implementation_guidance: Document security training requirements in your security policy. Use IAM to track user roles and required training. Maintain training records in a secure S3 bucket. Use CloudWatch Events to trigger training reminders. Integrate with your LMS or use AWS Partner solutions for training delivery.

gv.po-01.title: Document audit logging policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented audit policy that defines CloudTrail requirements, log retention, and review procedures.

gv.po-01.title: Centralize logs with AWS Organizations
gv.po-01.description: Use AWS Organizations to centralize CloudTrail logs from all accounts into a dedicated logging account.

gv.po-01.title: Implement log retention policies
gv.po-01.description: Configure CloudWatch Logs retention periods and S3 lifecycle policies to meet audit log retention requirements.

gv.po-01.title: Document configuration management policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented configuration management policy requiring Infrastructure as Code and AWS Config for compliance monitoring.

gv.po-01.title: Enable AWS Config in all regions
gv.po-01.description: Enable AWS Config in all regions to track configuration changes and maintain configuration history.

gv.po-01.title: Use CloudFormation or Terraform for IaC
gv.po-01.description: Implement Infrastructure as Code using CloudFormation or Terraform to ensure consistent and auditable infrastructure deployments.

gv.po-01.title: Document disaster recovery policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented contingency planning policy that defines RTO/RPO requirements and AWS Backup strategies.

gv.po-01.title: Implement AWS Backup centralized management
gv.po-01.description: Use AWS Backup to centrally manage and automate backups across AWS services.

gv.po-01.title: Configure multi-region replication
gv.po-01.description: Implement cross-region replication for critical data using S3 replication, RDS read replicas, or DynamoDB global tables.

gv.po-01.title: Document authentication policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented authentication policy requiring MFA, password complexity, and federated access via SSO.

gv.po-01.title: Implement IAM Identity Center for SSO
gv.po-01.description: Use AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO) to provide centralized authentication and federated access.

gv.po-01.title: Enforce IAM password policy
gv.po-01.description: Configure IAM password policy with minimum length, complexity requirements, and rotation periods.

gv.po-01.title: Document incident response policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented incident response policy that defines AWS-specific incident handling procedures and escalation paths.

gv.po-01.title: Enable Security Hub for incident aggregation
gv.po-01.description: Use Security Hub to aggregate security findings from multiple AWS services for centralized incident detection.

gv.po-01.title: Configure EventBridge for incident automation
gv.po-01.description: Use Amazon EventBridge to automate incident response actions based on Security Hub findings and GuardDuty alerts.

gv.po-01.title: Document encryption policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented encryption policy requiring TLS 1.2+ for data in transit and KMS encryption for data at rest.

gv.po-01.title: Enforce encryption with SCPs
gv.po-01.description: Use Service Control Policies to enforce encryption requirements across all AWS accounts in the organization.

gv.po-01.title: Monitor encryption compliance with Config
gv.po-01.description: Use AWS Config rules to continuously monitor encryption compliance for S3, EBS, RDS, and other services.

gv.po-01.title: Document security monitoring policy
gv.po-01.description: Maintain documented security monitoring policy that defines GuardDuty, Security Hub, and vulnerability scanning requirements.

gv.po-01.title: Enable GuardDuty in all regions
gv.po-01.description: Enable GuardDuty in all regions to continuously monitor for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior.

gv.po-01.title: Enable Inspector for vulnerability scanning
gv.po-01.description: Enable Amazon Inspector to automatically scan EC2 instances, Lambda functions, and container images for vulnerabilities.

pr.aa-06.title: Media Storage

Document chain of custody procedures.

rs.mi-01.description: Configure CloudWatch alarms to detect and alert on security-relevant events for rapid incident response.

rs.mi-01.title: Remove unused Amazon EC2 security groups
rs.mi-01.description: Review and remove unused EC2 security groups to reduce complexity in network management and improve network security and performance.

rs.mi-01.title: Do not use default security group rules in Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
rs.mi-01.description: Change VPC default security groups rules from their default configuration and ensure defaults are not used to prevent unrestricted inbound/outbound traffic.

rs.mi-01.title: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones
rs.mi-01.description: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones to provide redundancy, scalability and improved availability.

rs.mi-01.title: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in AWS Network Firewall policies
rs.mi-01.description: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in Network Firewall policies that match defined control parameters.

rs.mi-01.title: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancers
rs.mi-01.description: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancer to customize security settings according to specific requirements and specific cipher standards.

rs.mi-01.title: Enable EKS Runtime Monitoring in Amazon GuardDuty
rs.mi-01.description: Activate Amazon EKS Runtime Monitoring with automated agent management in GuardDuty to detect runtime security threats in Kubernetes clusters.

rs.mi-01.title: Identify non-archived security findings in Amazon GuardDuty
rs.mi-01.description: Identify non-archived security findings (low/medium/high severity) in Amazon GuardDuty that exceed the configured number of days.

rs.mi-01.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
rs.mi-01.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.

rs.mi-02.title: Enable Amazon GuardDuty for threat detection
rs.mi-02.description: Enable GuardDuty to continuously monitor for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior to protect AWS accounts, workloads, and data.

rs.mi-02.title: Enable AWS Security Hub for centralized security findings
rs.mi-02.description: Enable Security Hub to aggregate, organize, and prioritize security findings from multiple AWS services and third-party products.

rs.mi-02.title: Configure CloudWatch alarms for security events
rs.mi-02.description: Configure CloudWatch alarms to detect and alert on security-relevant events for rapid incident response.

rs.mi-02.title: Automate patching with Systems Manager Patch Manager
rs.mi-02.description: Use Systems Manager Patch Manager to automate the process of patching managed instances with security-related updates.

rs.mi-02.title: Enable automatic minor version upgrades for RDS
rs.mi-02.description: Enable automatic minor version upgrades for RDS database instances to ensure timely application of security patches.

rs.mi-02.title: Use latest Lambda runtime versions
rs.mi-02.description: Ensure Lambda functions use the latest runtime versions to benefit from security patches and improvements.

rs.mi-02.title: Prevent unauthorized changes to AWS Config settings
rs.mi-02.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or disabling AWS Config settings.

rs.mi-02.title: Send event notifications from AWS CloudFormation stacks to an Amazon SNS topic
rs.mi-02.description: Send event notifications from CloudFormation stacks to an SNS topic to notify personnel of potential security events.

rs.mi-02.title: Prevent unauthorized modifications to AWS Config Rules managed by Control Tower
rs.mi-02.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or deleting AWS Config rules.
